compatibility_report:
# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues: Marvel’s Spider-Man Remastered
- Steam AppID of the game: 1817070

## System Information
- GPU: NVIDIA GeForce RTX 2070 SUPER
- Driver/LLVM version: nvidia 515.65.01
- Kernel version: 5.18.0-0.bpo.1-amd64
- Link to full system information report as [Gist](https://gist.github.com/): https://gist.github.com/sysms/2137da52097908629323004c546c845b
- Proton version: Experimental & 7.0.3

## I confirm:
- [X ] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [ X] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

<!-- Please add `PROTON_LOG=1 %command%` to the game's launch options and
attach the generated $HOME/steam-$APPID.log to this issue report as a file.
(Proton logs compress well if needed.)-->

## Symptoms <!-- What's the problem? -->

After starting the game, a first window appears, telling me, I've no GPU installed.
Clicking on OK starts the launcher, which works well. Tried different graphical presets (low, medium).
After this, another window tells me, I've not the latest version of graphics driver.
Clicking on OK, a black screen appears, and after some seconds, the game crashes.
Sometimes the crash reporter appears, sometimes it doesn't.

Game works well on my Steam Deck. :) 

## Reproduction

Always.

<!--
1. You can find the Steam AppID in the URL of the shop page of the game.
   e.g. for `The Witcher 3: Wild Hunt` the AppID is `292030`.
2. You can find your driver and Linux version, as well as your graphics
   processor's name in the system information report of Steam.
3. You can retrieve a full system information report by clicking
   `Help` > `System Information` in the Steam client on your machine.
4. Please copy it to your clipboard by pressing `Ctrl+A` and then `Ctrl+C`.
   Then paste it in a [Gist](https://gist.github.com/) and post the link in
   this issue.
5. Please search for open issues and pull requests by the name of the game and
   find out whether they are relevant and should be referenced above.
-->
[steam-1817070.log](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/9328634/steam-1817070.log)
![spiderbug3](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/4120422/184406581-8ab7ec0a-fd3a-4660-a2f2-cde60dfa79cf.png)
![spiderbug2](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/4120422/184406587-a12c4d60-0e5b-4949-aa08-59d93b9fbb96.png)
![spiderbug1](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/4120422/184406589-a4efa4c0-33e5-4f87-9e57-be77644daf19.png)

compatibility_report:
# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues: GUG Demo
- Steam AppID of the game: 3256600

## System Information
- Steam Deck (LCD)
- Video driver version: 4.6 Mesa 24.3.4 (git-8d0948917e)
- Kernel version: 6.11.11
- Proton version: 1763652710 experimental-10.0-20251120

## I confirm:
- [x] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [x] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

## Symptoms <!-- What's the problem? -->

Shows a white screen, menu music can be heard, moving right analog stick shows the cursor. Clicking L2/L3 triggers a crash.

[steam-3256600(nod3dcompilerdll_buttonscrash).log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/23699555/steam-3256600.nod3dcompilerdll_buttonscrash.log)

Alt+F4 triggers another crash with a Unity crash screen.

[steam-3256600(nod3dcompilerdll_altf4crash).log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/23699557/steam-3256600.nod3dcompilerdll_altf4crash.log)

After adding d3dcompiler_47.dll through protontricks it still shows a white screen, but doesn't crash after clicking L2/L3 anymore. Moving the cursor around shows some graphics over the white screen:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/aa91d684-a6dc-4e96-830b-be9002826309)

Also the "Start" button shows the menus with all the graphics:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/a4b1091e-2a08-43c2-8ded-6daeb3bf89eb)

 Alt+F4 still triggers the crash.

[steam-3256600(withd3dcompilerdll_altf4crash).log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/23699580/steam-3256600.withd3dcompilerdll_altf4crash.log)

## Reproduction

Install and run the game.
symptom_summary:
用户在运行GUG Demo时遇到的问题包括显示白屏，虽然可以听到菜单音乐，但点击L2/L3会导致崩溃。使用Alt+F4也会触发崩溃，尽管添加了d3dcompiler_47.dll后不再崩溃，但仍然只显示白屏，偶尔会显示一些图形。

**Tag Suggestions:** White Screen, Crashes, Visual Glitches, Audio Present

compatibility_report:
# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues: Soul Hackers 2
- Steam AppID of the game: 1777620

## System Information
- GPU: AMD Radeon RX 5700
- Driver/LLVM version: Mesa 22.1.7
- Kernel version: 5.19.6-200.fc36.x86_64
- Link to full system information report as [Gist](https://gist.github.com/): https://gist.github.com/crebwyllion/5625297796fd914f46e81265da6d51f5
- Proton version: Proton Experimental

## I confirm:
- [x] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [x] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

<!-- Please add `PROTON_LOG=1 %command%` to the game's launch options and
attach the generated $HOME/steam-$APPID.log to this issue report as a file.
(Proton logs compress well if needed.)-->

## Symptoms <!-- What's the problem? -->
Certain spell effects, textures, and other such things are missing, Ringo's blade slash outside of combat being the easiest to test and see. Other people have also reported issues with fullscreen, but that's outside the purview of this report.

[steam-1777620.log](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/9484280/steam-1777620.log)
## Reproduction
Load the game, start a new file, and play through until you reach a scene where it's suppose to be raining ([shown here](https://youtu.be/I7IFlEJ5eM4?t=1015)). There will be water dripping down the screen, but no raindrops ([shown here](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/85197579/188305343-bd755602-4400-43f1-aae4-33d04a2637d7.png)). A more obvious example would be a cutscene not much later where there should be triangles floating across the screen ([shown here](https://youtu.be/I7IFlEJ5eM4?t=1578)), but there won't be on Proton ([shown here](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/85197579/188305309-e0adddac-b11f-4716-90f6-11b965e48a66.png)).

<!--
1. You can find the Steam AppID in the URL of the shop page of the game.
   e.g. for `The Witcher 3: Wild Hunt` the AppID is `292030`.
2. You can find your driver and Linux version, as well as your graphics
   processor's name in the system information report of Steam.
3. You can retrieve a full system information report by clicking
   `Help` > `System Information` in the Steam client on your machine.
4. Please copy it to your clipboard by pressing `Ctrl+A` and then `Ctrl+C`.
   Then paste it in a [Gist](https://gist.github.com/) and post the link in
   this issue.
5. Please search for open issues and pull requests by the name of the game and
   find out whether they are relevant and should be referenced above.
-->

compatibility_report:
# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues: Sackboy: A Big Adventure 1599660
- Steam AppID of the game: 1599660

## System Information
- GPU: Radeon RX 6800
- Video driver version: Mesa 23.2.1
- Kernel version: 6.5.11
- Proton version: 8.0-4

## I confirm:
- [x] that I haven't found an existing (open) compatibility report for this game.
- [x] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

## Symptoms <!-- What's the problem? -->

When launching the game there is always a default Keyboard and Mouse player 1 so there is no way to play player 1 with a controller effectively making the game for single players keyboard and mouse only and in koop one has still to play with the keyboard. I haven't tried this yet but unplugging the keyboard might fix this... naturally just unplugging the keyboard to play a game is not the way it should be intended to be. (This might also be the reason why steam deck users to not encounter this issue since they do not have a keyboard that could be detected and/or unplugged)

Inside the settings there is a button for the keyboard to change it's user slot and therefore theoretically switch the first position with a controller making it playable with a controller but the button is set to none and it cannot be changed neither ingame nor in any configuration file.
symptom_summary:
用户在启动游戏时，始终会默认使用键盘和鼠标作为玩家1，导致无法有效地使用控制器进行游戏，单人模式只能使用键盘和鼠标。设置中虽然有更改用户插槽的选项，但该按钮无法更改，无法实现控制器的使用。

Tag suggestions: 
- Input Issues
- Controller Support
- Configuration Problems

## Symptoms 
Missing character and world textures immediately after launching into the game and completing the initial in-game menu setup. I launched the game under gamescope with `PROTON_LOG=1 gamescope -e -f -h 2160 -w 7680 -r 240 --hdr-enabled --mangoapp --force-grab-cursor -- %command%`

compatibility_report:
# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues: Insurgency: Sandstorm
- Steam AppID of the game: 581320

## System Information
- GPU:  GTX 1070 
- Driver/LLVM version: LLVM 13.0.1 / nvidia 470.103.01
- Kernel version: 5.15.26
- Link to full system information report as [Gist](https://gist.github.com/Irets/13e76a5afcbcabcd0cd434f5fed12aaf):
- Proton version: 1646943044 experimental-7.0-20220310
[steam-581320.log](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/8227824/steam-581320.log)

## I confirm:
- [ ] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [ ] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

<!-- Please add `PROTON_LOG=1 %command%` to the game's launch options and
attach the generated $HOME/steam-$APPID.log to this issue report as a file.
(Proton logs compress well if needed.)-->

## Symptoms <!-- What's the problem? -->
Upon launching Insurgency: Sandstorm, an EasyAntiCheat splash screen will pop up saying "Launch Error, Failed to load the anti-cheat module" and at this point the only way forward is to click the "Exit" button below the message.

I've tackled with this issue for about a week now and have found a probable source of the problem:
**easyanticheat_linux_x86_x64.eac.metadata** and **easyanticheat_linux_x86_x64.eac** are missing from _~/.steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/581320/pfx/drive_c/users/steamuser/AppData/Roaming/EasyAntiCheat/eb330869cd5f4881b0a8a0220a8c284d/2324da9a1c0341eea4cf43c070760c75/_.

From my understanding these files should be present after the EAC installer has done it's job when Steam is running the installscript on a fresh prefix, but they never appear, thus causing EAC to bail on game start.

To try to solve this issue, I've tried the following:

- Move Proton Experimental, Proton EasyAntiCheat Runtime and Insurgency:Sandstorm to a ext4 filesystem and reinstall all these titles & tools through Steam. 
- Opt into Steam Experimental Bleeding Edge beta branch 
- Try Proton 7.0 & GE-Proton7-9 
- Wipe the Proton prefix & shadercache folders numerous times
- Copy the missing **easyanticheat_linux_x86_x64.eac.metadata** and **easyanticheat_linux_x86_x64.eac** on to my system from my friend's PC, but EAC will delete these files on game start.
- Copy the whole Proton prefix from my friend's computer and place into _~/.steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/_
- Attempt to invoke the **EasyAntiCheat_EOS_Setup.exe** with Proton Experimental manually, only to be faced by an empty command prompt which will close shortly after.
- Install Insurgency: Sandstorm on my laptop that resulted in the same issue as in this Issue Ticket.



## Reproduction

- Install Proton EasyAntiCheat Runtime, Proton Experimental and Insurgency: Sandstorm
- Proceed to launch Insurgency: Sandstorm
##
I will include **service.log**, **anticheatlauncher.log** and **loader.log** from _~/.steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/581320/pfx/drive_c/users/steamuser/AppData/Roaming/EasyAntiCheat_
[service.log](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/8227929/service.log)
[anticheatlauncher.log](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/8227930/anticheatlauncher.log)
[loader.log](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/8227932/loader.log)

Is this the right place to ask for support or report the issue?
I'm not very familar with Github.

Cheers.

<!--
1. You can find the Steam AppID in the URL of the shop page of the game.
   e.g. for `The Witcher 3: Wild Hunt` the AppID is `292030`.
2. You can find your driver and Linux version, as well as your graphics
   processor's name in the system information report of Steam.
3. You can retrieve a full system information report by clicking
   `Help` > `System Information` in the Steam client on your machine.
4. Please copy it to your clipboard by pressing `Ctrl+A` and then `Ctrl+C`.
   Then paste it in a [Gist](https://gist.github.com/) and post the link in
   this issue.
5. Please search for open issues and pull requests by the name of the game and
   find out whether they are relevant and should be referenced above.
-->

compatibility_report:
# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues: LunarLux
- Steam AppID of the game: 1932680

## System Information
- Link to full system information report as [Gist](https://gist.github.com/AlleyDismay/f8821eaa89436d1d8ad0fc5cc8ac7d2b):

## I confirm:
- [X] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [X] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

[steam-1932680 - proton-7.0-6e ctd.log](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/13465176/steam-1932680.-.proton-7.0-6e.ctd.log)
[steam-1932680 - Shortest 6.3 Steam Overlay CTD.log](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/13465256/steam-1932680.-.Shortest.6.3.Steam.Overlay.CTD.log)


## Symptoms

Crashes after the publisher logo in Proton >=7.0-6e. Goes to start screen and beyond on lower versions. Making notes on Steam Overlay is really laggy and the NumLock doesn't work in it.

Only happened with this keyboard of mine as `lsub` reports:

`Bus 003 Device 007: ID 413c:2010 Dell Computer Corp. Keyboard` - SK-8135

My other keyboard was:

`Bus 003 Device 006: ID 04b3:301b IBM Corp. SK-8815 Keyboard` - SK-8815

..................

Sometimes it crashes when I try to use the Steam Overlay:

[steam-1932680 - Crashed on trying to Steam Overlay.log](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/13465224/steam-1932680.-.Crashed.on.trying.to.Steam.Overlay.log)

## Reproduction

Run the game in a Proton version 7.0-6e or higher, that's not Experimental. Experimental [bleeding-edge] as of Nov 28 10:29 EST seems to work fine, sometimes. Excepting this Overlay Crash:

[steam-1932680 - Experimental Seems to work, but Overlay CTD.log](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/13495794/steam-1932680.-.Experimental.Seems.to.work.but.Overlay.CTD.log)

And this one on next bootup attempt, but after that is fine?
[steam-1932680 - CTD Experimental Spinner Keyboard.log](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/13495809/steam-1932680.-.CTD.Experimental.Spinner.Keyboard.log)
symptom_summary:

**Tags:** White Screen, Unresponsive, Launch Issue
